summary: Surface rows.Scan / rows.Err / Columns / MarshalIndent errors in handleSQL instead of swallowing them.
reason: A swallowed rows.Scan error left every column at the zero value and the row serialized as all-null entries — indistinguishable from a legitimately-empty result row to an agent calling the MCP sql tool. Same pattern existed for rows.Columns and the final MarshalIndent. The MCP surface is the boundary where these silent failures would matter most because the agent can't see the underlying driver state.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/mcp -count=1 still passes (no behavior change on the happy path); a future regression test can drive a malformed value into Scan to assert the new error result, but the change is mechanical enough that a unit test would mostly be testing the stdlib.
